channel.title: Gitea Limited — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by Gitea Limited (gitea).

item.title: CVE-2026-20912 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea: Cross-Repository Authorization Bypass via Release Attachment Linking Leads to Private Attachment Disclosure
item.description: Gitea does not properly validate repository ownership when linking attachments to releases. An attachment uploaded to a private repository could potentially be linked to a release in a different public repository, making it accessible to unauthorized…

item.title: CVE-2026-20904 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Gitea: Broken access control in OpenID visibility toggle enables cross-user visibility changes
item.description: Gitea does not properly validate ownership when toggling OpenID URI visibility. An authenticated user may be able to change the visibility settings of other users' OpenID identities.

item.title: CVE-2026-20897 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea Git LFS Lock Deletion Broken Access Control (Cross-Repo IDOR)
item.description: Gitea does not properly validate repository ownership when deleting Git LFS locks. A user with write access to one repository may be able to delete LFS locks belonging to other repositories.

item.title: CVE-2026-20888 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Gitea Pull Requests Auto-Merge: Read-Only Users Can Cancel Scheduled Auto-Merge via Web Endpoint (Authorization Bypass)
item.description: Gitea does not properly verify authorization when canceling scheduled auto-merges via the web interface. A user with read access to pull requests may be able to cancel auto-merges scheduled by other users.

item.title: CVE-2026-20883 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Gitea Stopwatch API Missing Authorization Check Leads to Post-Revocation Information Disclosure
item.description: Gitea's stopwatch API does not re-validate repository access permissions. After a user's access to a private repository is revoked, they may still view issue titles and repository names through previously started stopwatches.

item.title: CVE-2026-20800 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Notification API Leaks Private Repository Issue Titles After Collaborator Permission Revocation
item.description: Gitea's notification API does not re-validate repository access permissions when returning notification details. After a user's access to a private repository is revoked, they may still view issue and pull request titles through previously received n…

item.title: CVE-2026-20750 · 9.1 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Gitea Organization Projects Cross-Organization Authorization Bypass via Project ID (IDOR)
item.description: Gitea does not properly validate project ownership in organization project operations. A user with project write access in one organization may be able to modify projects belonging to a different organization.

item.title: CVE-2026-20736 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Gitea Web Attachment Deletion: Cross-Repository Unauthorized Deletion via Missing Repo Ownership Check
item.description: Gitea does not properly verify repository context when deleting attachments. A user who previously uploaded an attachment to a repository may be able to delete it after losing access to that repository by making the request through a different reposi…

item.title: CVE-2026-0798 · 3.5 LOW — Gitea Release Email Notifications Leak Private Repository Release Details After Access Revocation
item.description: Gitea may send release notification emails for private repositories to users whose access has been revoked. When a repository is changed from public to private, users who previously watched the repository may continue to receive release notifications…

item.title: CVE-2024-6886 · 10 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Inproper Sanitation of field leading to stored XSS
item.description: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (XSS or 'Cross-site Scripting') vulnerability in Gitea Gitea Open Source Git Server allows Stored XSS.This issue affects Gitea Open Source Git Server: 1.22.0.
